tests.description: Every entry has a non-empty human label for its card.
tests.key: ADMIN_COLLECTIONS › gives every collection a non-empty label

tests.description: The dashboard covers the five folder collections, in display order.
tests.key: ADMIN_COLLECTIONS › lists the five editable folder collections in order

tests.description: The inline gate script and the helper must agree on the sessionStorage key.
tests.key: ADMIN_GATE_STORAGE_KEY › is the stable admin-unlocked key

tests.description: Clicking the button increments the hydrated counter, proving the island is genuinely interactive and not a static render.
tests.key: Greeting › increments the counter on click

tests.description: The subtitle is optional: when omitted the island must not render an empty paragraph, which is what the "missing optional prop" scenario exercises.
tests.key: Greeting › omits the subtitle when no subtitle prop is given

tests.description: The headline must include the provided name so the island renders the prop, not a hardcoded string.
tests.key: Greeting › renders the name in the headline

tests.description: A subject is appended as a query parameter.
tests.key: buildMailto › appends the subject as a query parameter

tests.description: With only a recipient, the result is a bare mailto: with no query string.
tests.key: buildMailto › builds a bare mailto when only a recipient is given

tests.description: Spaces in the subject must encode as %20, not +, for mail clients.
tests.key: buildMailto › encodes spaces in the subject as %20 rather than plus

tests.description: A body alone (no subject) still produces a valid query string.
tests.key: buildMailto › handles a body with no subject

tests.description: Both subject and body are included in the query string.
tests.key: buildMailto › includes both subject and body when supplied

tests.description: The marker-change live signal even overrides a failed run phase.
tests.key: computeBanner › a marker change wins even when the run reports failed

tests.description: A deploy running past the slow threshold escalates to the slow state.
tests.key: computeBanner › escalates to slow past the threshold

tests.description: A marker commit differing from baseline is the authoritative live signal.
tests.key: computeBanner › flips to live once the marker commit differs from baseline and wins over everything

tests.description: An in-progress Actions run upgrades the banner to building.
tests.key: computeBanner › shows building when the Actions run is in progress

tests.description: Just after a save with no marker change and no run info, it is deploying.
tests.key: computeBanner › shows deploying right after a save with marker unchanged and no run info

tests.description: A failed run with an unchanged marker surfaces the failed state and a logs link.
tests.key: computeBanner › shows failed when the run failed and the marker has not moved

tests.description: With no active save the banner is idle and reports the last deploy time.
tests.key: computeBanner › shows idle with last-deployed time when there is no active save

tests.description: a production build (no override) reads the committed source dir
tests.key: contentRoot › defaults to src/content when the override is unset

tests.description: when the editor points the app at a sandbox, the override wins
tests.key: contentRoot › returns CODEYAM_CONTENT_ROOT when it is set

tests.description: Zero entries shows the empty-state nudge, not a "0 entries" sentence.
tests.key: dashboardSummaryText › shows the empty-state nudge when there is no content

tests.description: The populated summary reports total entries and collection count.
tests.key: dashboardSummaryText › summarizes a populated dashboard

tests.description: A negative total is treated as empty, guarding against bad input.
tests.key: dashboardSummaryText › treats a negative total as empty

tests.description: A single entry uses the singular "entry".
tests.key: dashboardSummaryText › uses the singular entry for a count of one

tests.description: Collections with a zero count still count toward collectionCount but add nothing.
tests.key: dashboardTotals › counts empty collections without adding to the total

tests.description: An empty card list totals zero across zero collections.
tests.key: dashboardTotals › returns zeros for an empty card list

tests.description: Sums every card's count and reports how many collections there are.
tests.key: dashboardTotals › sums counts and reports the collection count

tests.description: a production build (no override) reads the committed data dir
tests.key: dataRoot › defaults to src/data when the override is unset

tests.description: the singleton data dir follows the same sandbox redirect as content
tests.key: dataRoot › returns CODEYAM_DATA_ROOT when it is set

tests.description: A path with no /admin segment has no derivable base.
tests.key: deployBaseFromPath › falls back to empty base when there is no admin segment

tests.description: Custom-domain root: the dashboard sits at base ''.
tests.key: deployBaseFromPath › returns empty base for the custom-domain dashboard path

tests.description: Custom-domain root: the editor sits at base ''.
tests.key: deployBaseFromPath › returns empty base for the custom-domain editor path

tests.description: Project-subpath mode: everything before /admin is the base for the dashboard path.
tests.key: deployBaseFromPath › returns the subpath base for the project-subpath dashboard path

tests.description: Project-subpath mode: everything before /admin is the base for the editor path.
tests.key: deployBaseFromPath › returns the subpath base for the project-subpath editor path

tests.description: an explicitly active member is kept
tests.key: filterActiveBoardMembers › keeps a member with active: true

tests.description: a member with no active field defaults to shown (backward-compatible)
tests.key: filterActiveBoardMembers › keeps a member with no active field

tests.description: mixed list keeps only the non-false members, order preserved
tests.key: filterActiveBoardMembers › keeps active and undefined members while dropping inactive ones

tests.description: an explicitly inactive member is removed
tests.key: filterActiveBoardMembers › removes a member with active: false

tests.description: does not mutate the caller's array
tests.key: filterActiveBoardMembers › returns a new array without mutating the input

tests.description: an all-inactive list returns empty (board falls back to the graphic)
tests.key: filterActiveBoardMembers › returns an empty array when every member is inactive

tests.description: Single-digit days are not zero-padded.
tests.key: formatEventDate › does not zero-pad single-digit days

tests.description: A Date instance formats the same way as the equivalent string.
tests.key: formatEventDate › formats a Date instance the same as its ISO string

tests.description: A UTC-midnight ISO string formats to a long en-US date.
tests.key: formatEventDate › formats an ISO date as Month D, YYYY

tests.description: Capable browser, no reduced-motion preference: animate the tiles in.
tests.key: galleryRevealImmediately › animates when IntersectionObserver exists and motion is allowed

tests.description: No IntersectionObserver (old browser / SSR) → reveal immediately so the gallery never gets stuck invisible.
tests.key: galleryRevealImmediately › reveals immediately when IntersectionObserver is unavailable

tests.description: Both unfavorable → still reveal immediately.
tests.key: galleryRevealImmediately › reveals immediately when both reduced motion and no observer

tests.description: Reduced-motion preference wins even on a capable browser.
tests.key: galleryRevealImmediately › reveals immediately when the user prefers reduced motion

tests.description: A custom column count changes the wrap point.
tests.key: galleryStaggerDelay › respects a custom column count

tests.description: The first column has no delay.
tests.key: galleryStaggerDelay › returns zero for the first column

tests.description: Each column within a row adds 60ms.
tests.key: galleryStaggerDelay › staggers later columns by 60ms each

tests.description: The delay wraps per row, so tiles in the same column share a delay.
tests.key: galleryStaggerDelay › wraps the delay per row of five columns

tests.description: No gallery tiles on the page → a clean no-op, not a throw.
tests.key: initGalleryReveal › is a no-op when there are no gallery tiles

tests.description: Capable browser, motion allowed: tiles are observed, not revealed up front.
tests.key: initGalleryReveal › observes tiles instead of revealing them when animation is enabled

tests.description: Reduced motion: every tile is revealed immediately (in-view), no observer.
tests.key: initGalleryReveal › reveals every tile immediately under reduced motion

tests.description: No IntersectionObserver: fall back to revealing every tile immediately.
tests.key: initGalleryReveal › reveals every tile immediately when IntersectionObserver is missing

tests.description: No [data-parallax] images on the page → a clean no-op, not a throw.
tests.key: initParallax › is a no-op when there are no parallax images

tests.description: Touch / coarse pointer: parallax never activates, so the image keeps its default static cover styling (no height override, no transform).
tests.key: initParallax › leaves the image untouched on a non-fine-hover device

tests.description: Reduced-motion preference wins even on a capable desktop: image stays static.
tests.key: initParallax › leaves the image untouched when the user prefers reduced motion

tests.description: Desktop (fine hover, no reduced motion): the image is sized to the viewport and pinned via a transform cancelling the section's offset from the top.
tests.key: initParallax › pins the banner image to the viewport on a fine-hover desktop
initReveal › is a no-op when there are no reveal elements
initReveal › observes elements instead of revealing them when animation is enabled
initReveal › reveals every element immediately under reduced motion
initReveal › reveals every element immediately when IntersectionObserver is missing

tests.description: Surrounding and repeated whitespace is collapsed rather than producing blank initials.
tests.key: initials › ignores surrounding and repeated whitespace

tests.description: An empty name yields an empty initials string.
tests.key: initials › returns an empty string for an empty name

tests.description: A single-word name yields a single initial.
tests.key: initials › returns one letter for a single-word name

tests.description: initials takes the first letter of the first two words and upper-cases them.
tests.key: initials › returns two-letter initials for a two-word name

tests.description: Lowercase names are upper-cased in the initials.
tests.key: initials › upper-cases lowercase names

tests.description: Only the first two words contribute to the initials.
tests.key: initials › uses only the first two words for a three-word name

tests.description: The happy path: an exact match unlocks.
tests.key: isPasscodeCorrect › accepts an exact match

tests.description: The comparison is case-sensitive (a deterrent, but not a careless one).
tests.key: isPasscodeCorrect › is case-sensitive

tests.description: An empty expected passcode never matches — a blank build value must not unlock the dashboard for everyone.
tests.key: isPasscodeCorrect › never matches an empty expected passcode

tests.description: A wrong value is rejected.
tests.key: isPasscodeCorrect › rejects a wrong value

tests.description: Surrounding whitespace on the expected passcode is trimmed too.
tests.key: isPasscodeCorrect › trims surrounding whitespace on the expected value

tests.description: Surrounding whitespace on the input is trimmed before comparing.
tests.key: isPasscodeCorrect › trims surrounding whitespace on the input

tests.description: Guards the wrap-once contract: authored data stays base-agnostic and `withBase` is applied a single time at the render site, never re-fed.
tests.key: landing image base paths › does not double-prefix a path that already carries the base

tests.description: At the domain root (base `/`) the same paths must pass through untouched, so a future custom-domain deploy doesn't get a doubled or broken prefix.
tests.key: landing image base paths › leaves every internal landing image unchanged at the domain root

tests.description: Under the Pages base `/harvardintech/`, every landing asset must come back prefixed with the base — this is the exact bug (root-absolute src 404ing).
tests.key: landing image base paths › prefixes every internal landing image with the project base on a subpath deploy

tests.description: A completed failure is the failed phase.
tests.key: mapRunPhase › maps a completed failure to failed

tests.description: A completed success is the succeeded phase.
tests.key: mapRunPhase › maps a completed success to succeeded

tests.description: A missing run is unknown, never a phase.
tests.key: mapRunPhase › maps a missing run to unknown

tests.description: A queued run has not started executing yet, so it is building.
tests.key: mapRunPhase › maps a queued run to building

tests.description: A timed-out completion is treated as a failure.
tests.key: mapRunPhase › maps a timed_out completion to failed

tests.description: An in-progress run is still building.
tests.key: mapRunPhase › maps an in-progress run to building

tests.description: A cancelled completion is unknown so it never masquerades as a real failure.
tests.key: mapRunPhase › treats a cancelled completion as unknown, not failed

tests.description: At the empty base the marker and view URLs are domain-rooted.
tests.key: markerUrl / viewSiteUrl › builds root marker and view URLs at the empty base

tests.description: Under a subpath base both URLs are prefixed with the base.
tests.key: markerUrl / viewSiteUrl › builds subpath marker and view URLs

tests.description: Both unfavorable → off.
tests.key: parallaxEnabled › disables parallax for touch devices with reduced motion

tests.description: Touch / coarse pointer → off, regardless of motion preference.
tests.key: parallaxEnabled › disables parallax on non-fine-hover touch devices

tests.description: Reduced-motion preference wins even on a capable desktop.
tests.key: parallaxEnabled › disables parallax when the user prefers reduced motion

tests.description: Desktop: fine pointer + hover, no reduced-motion preference → parallax on.
tests.key: parallaxEnabled › enables parallax for fine-hover devices without reduced motion

tests.description: A section still below the viewport top (positive rect.top) is pulled UP by the same amount, so the image reads as fixed to the viewport.
tests.key: parallaxOffset › returns a negative offset for a section below the viewport top

tests.description: Once the section has scrolled above the viewport top (negative rect.top), the image is pushed DOWN to stay pinned.
tests.key: parallaxOffset › returns a positive offset for a section scrolled above the viewport top

tests.description: Flush with the viewport top: no translation needed. (`-0 === 0` is true; we compare numerically rather than via Object.is to ignore the sign of zero.)
tests.key: parallaxOffset › returns zero when the section is flush with the viewport top

tests.description: An absent branch defaults to main, matching the CMS default.
tests.key: parseBackendRepo › defaults the branch to main when omitted

tests.description: The repo and branch are pulled straight out of the CMS config text.
tests.key: parseBackendRepo › pulls repo and branch out of the CMS config

tests.description: No repo means no Actions target, so the parse returns null.
tests.key: parseBackendRepo › returns null when no repo is configured

tests.description: The most recent deploy-workflow run wins, ignoring other workflows entirely.
tests.key: pickLatestDeployRun › picks the most recent run for the deploy workflow, skipping other workflows

tests.description: No run for the requested workflow path yields null.
tests.key: pickLatestDeployRun › returns null when no run matches the workflow path

tests.description: Past a day it rolls up to days.
tests.key: relativeTime › reports days

tests.description: Past an hour it rolls up to hours.
tests.key: relativeTime › reports hours

tests.description: Under a minute reads as just now.
tests.key: relativeTime › reports just now under a minute

tests.description: Several minutes use the plural unit.
tests.key: relativeTime › reports plural minutes

tests.description: Exactly one minute uses the singular unit.
tests.key: relativeTime › reports singular minute

tests.description: settings + nav are read from the data root and parsed from JSON, so a sandbox data dir fully drives what the site renders

tests.description: a different data root yields different values — the loader is not pinned to a single committed file (the sandbox isolation guarantee at the read side)
tests.key: site singletons › reflects a second data root on re-import

tests.description: The synthesized e-mail entry resolves to the envelope icon.
tests.key: socialIconSrc › maps email to its self-hosted asset

tests.description: Facebook resolves to the downloaded facebook icon.
tests.key: socialIconSrc › maps facebook to its self-hosted asset

tests.description: Each known platform maps to its self-hosted asset under public/images/social/.
tests.key: socialIconSrc › maps twitter to its self-hosted asset

tests.description: Lookup is case-insensitive so data-layer casing variations still resolve.
tests.key: socialIconSrc › resolves regardless of icon casing

tests.description: An unknown platform returns null so the caller degrades to label-only.
tests.key: socialIconSrc › returns null for an unknown icon

tests.description: A missing icon value returns null rather than throwing or guessing.
tests.key: socialIconSrc › returns null for null, undefined, or empty input

tests.description: Members are ordered by ascending order field.
tests.key: sortBoardMembers › orders members by ascending order value

tests.description: Sorting returns a new array and does not mutate the caller's input.
tests.key: sortBoardMembers › returns a new array without mutating the input

tests.description: An empty member list returns an empty array.
tests.key: sortBoardMembers › returns an empty array for no members

tests.description: A member with no order is treated as order 0 and sorts first.
tests.key: sortBoardMembers › treats a missing order as zero

tests.description: The original input array is not mutated by sorting.
tests.key: splitEvents › does not mutate the input array order

tests.description: An empty input yields empty upcoming and past lists.
tests.key: splitEvents › handles an empty event list

tests.description: When every event is in the future, past is empty.
tests.key: splitEvents › returns no past events when all are upcoming

tests.description: Events before `now` are past, sorted most recent first.
tests.key: splitEvents › returns past events sorted most recent first

tests.description: Events on or after `now` are upcoming, sorted soonest first.
tests.key: splitEvents › returns upcoming events sorted soonest first

tests.description: An event exactly at `now` counts as upcoming, not past.
tests.key: splitEvents › treats an event exactly at now as upcoming

tests.description: an empty role yields a single empty line, never throws
tests.key: splitRole › returns a single empty string for an empty role

tests.description: a role with no separator renders on a single line
tests.key: splitRole › returns a single-element array when there is no separator

tests.description: a role with a separator splits into title and Harvard class lines
tests.key: splitRole › splits a role on the middot into two lines

tests.description: more than one separator yields more than two lines
tests.key: splitRole › splits every occurrence of the separator

tests.description: surrounding whitespace around the separator is trimmed
tests.key: splitRole › trims whitespace around the separator

tests.description: An ISO date string is parsed into a Date.
tests.key: toEventDate › parses an ISO date string into a Date

tests.description: A Date instance passes through unchanged.
tests.key: toEventDate › returns a Date instance unchanged

tests.description: The bare root path stays '/' at the domain root.
tests.key: withBase › keeps the root path as a single slash when the base is root

tests.description: At the domain root the base is '/', so an internal path is returned unchanged.
tests.key: withBase › leaves an internal path unchanged when the base is root

tests.description: The root path becomes the base itself under a subpath, with no doubled slash.
tests.key: withBase › maps the root path to the base under a subpath

tests.description: A bare in-page anchor is not an absolute path, so it is left alone.
tests.key: withBase › passes a bare hash anchor through untouched

tests.description: Absolute external URLs are not internal paths and pass through untouched.
tests.key: withBase › passes external http URLs through untouched

tests.description: mailto and other non-slash schemes are left alone.
tests.key: withBase › passes mailto links through untouched

tests.description: Under a project subpath the base is prefixed onto the internal path.
tests.key: withBase › prefixes the subpath base onto an internal path

tests.description: A hash fragment on an absolute path is preserved after the base prefix.
tests.key: withBase › preserves a hash fragment on an absolute path under a subpath
